description: S105 cross-platform Kalshi↔Polymarket validation corpus scraping + candidate-generation infrastructure shipped via tools/build_cross_platform_corpus.py (470-line single-file CLI). Scrapes Kalshi /events?status=settled&with_nested_markets=true (politics/elections/economics/crypto/world, filtered client-side because the API ignores its own `category` param) + Polymarket Gamma /markets?closed=true&order=closedTime&ascending=false (no category filter — Polymarket no longer tags recent markets, rely on token-set Jaccard text similarity at pair-time). Live 3-day scrape: 735K unique Kalshi × 97K Polymarket → 604,603 candidate pairs (2,937 suggested-MATCH at Jaccard ≥0.60, 601,666 NEEDS_REVIEW at Jaccard 0.40–0.60). Two API quirks caught mid-implementation and regression-locked: Kalshi 5× duplication via category-param iteration (fixed: single-pass + client-side filter + ticker dedupe); Polymarket recent markets lack top-level category field (fixed: category filter removed). 49 pytest cases, no agent/ deps, no scanner/executor/strategy changes, no bot restart. Full pytest 1707/0 (1658 baseline + 49 net). Operator labeling protocol documented in CLAUDE.md Cross-platform corpus labeling protocol section.
